title: Tunisia World Cup Team Preview !
original_text:
Welcome to another team preview of another World Cup nation in Tunisia, these are a nation who are extremely hard to predict, and maybe this is going to be really hard for them as they have one of the hardest groups in this World Cup 2026.

As I see the Netherlands top the group, Japan 2nd, with Tunisia and Sweden battling out for the 3rd position and the playoffs, this is going to be a really competitive group this summer.

Coach and formation for Tunisia, which I am expecting them to play, is Sabri Lamouchi, the midfielder who used to play for many clubs, including Monaco, Parma, and Inter Milan, and now he is coaching at a high level, and the formation he plays is a 4-2-3-1 this is a possible starting 11 for lamouchi but as you see always there are other options with the players they have called up.

Ali Abdi - Currently playing for Nice in France and voted Tunisian Footballer of the Year for the last 2 years running. The left back at 32 will be a player worth watching at still 32 can perform at the top level and will be a defender to look out for this summer, and they could need that experience if they have any chance getting out of this World Cup group

The next player you might have heard of is his brother, Sami Khedira. Well, this is Rani Khedira, who recently chose to play for Tunisia and has been playing in the Bundesliga on the right wing for Union Berlin. He has played most of his football at Union Berlin, as well as at many clubs in the Bundesliga and played all the games for Union Berlin. At 32 brings more experience to the World Cup, playing in the top 5 leagues in Europe, so this is a player 100 per cent worth looking out for.

With Tunisia taking mainly a lot of defenders to the World Cup this summer, with 9 players, and Montassar Talbi, who is a 27-year-old centre back with over 60 caps for his country, and is a massive key player for his country, playing his football trade in France with FC Lorient, who are 9th in Ligue 1. He has possibly got 2 more World Cups left within him. At over 6ft, he has won over 64% aerial duels this season, and if he has a good World Cup, he could be looking for a big move this summer

As you can see from this graph above how good i think he will be in there defence and how they are going to need him if they are going to get out the group long passes in the 70 % range so he loves to bring the ball out from the back and also wins a lot of balls in the air so can be goo at corners and free kicks and you can tell that this is why he could be the leader for his country this summer !!!

They start their first game against Sweden at 3 am in the morning UK time, and all of their games are very early, but I will be watching them all, and also i am going to give a try to watch all the games to give my full analysis on all the games. But they have to beat Sweden or Japan as their final game is going to be a really hard game and cant see them beating Holland as i see them being and should be group winners. So my final outcome is 3rd in the group as i think i metion earlier

The heat which tunisia will be more used to than Sweden could be a factor especially as some of there players play mostly in the Premier League, where temperatures are completely different and lower than in the USA.

Thank you for reading another world cup article as i try and do better all the time, every time I do my football writing, and any feedback means a lot. If you could subscribe and like if you enjoyed it!
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
